title: The AI Public-Private Forum: Final report
summary: This Bank of England and FCA joint forum report examines barriers, challenges, and risks in AI adoption within UK financial services, organized around three levels: Data, Model Risk, and Governance. The report represents individual expert views rather than official institutional positions. Published February 2022, making it a historical document over four years old relative to today.
key_points: The AIPPF ran from October 2020 for one year with four quarterly meetings and workshops
Forum brought together experts from financial services, tech sector, and academia
Risks identified at three levels: Data, Model Risk, and Governance
All outputs reflect individual expert views, not official Bank of England or FCA positions
Report aims to advance understanding of safe AI adoption in financial services
Focus is on regulatory guidance and best practice for AI deployment, not monetary or macro topics
why_it_matters: This document is a fintech/regulatory forum report on AI adoption best practices. It does not address market structure or macro catalysts. It is not actionable for trading or investment decisions related to monetary policy, interest rates, or macroeconomic data.

issues_found: Source topic (AI adoption best practices in financial services) is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure
Document is over 4 years old (published February 2022) relative to TODAY (2026-03-31)
No actionable market, monetary policy, or macroeconomic information present

human_review.notes: The record is factually accurate and properly attributed, but the source is clearly outside the target scope of macro catalysts/market structure. Human decision needed on whether to accept with a scope-broadening rationale or reject for irrelevance.

notes: SOURCE IS NOT RELEVANT to the specified focus areas (market structure, macro catalysts). This is a regulatory/technology forum report on AI adoption in UK financial services.
